_comment: Golden-query regression set for knowledge_search (issue #62). Each query must be a PARAPHRASE of its expectedTitle entry's content — never a near-verbatim quote of the entry's title/content — otherwise retrieval passes trivially and the test asserts nothing. 'entries' includes distractor rows (topically adjacent, not targeted by any query) so the eval actually exercises near-duplicate shadowing. Queries marked core:true form the must-hit set (100% floor); all queries together form the aggregate set (>=80% floor). Fixtures are seeded/cleaned up per-run by tests/knowledgeEval.test.ts and must not depend on ambient DB contents. 'negativeQueries' (issue #95) are queries no entry actually answers — an 'off-topic' one and a topically-adjacent 'near-miss' one — used to ground KNOWLEDGE_SEARCH_RELEVANCE_THRESHOLD (src/agent/tools.ts) empirically against this fixture's own entries and guard against embedding-model drift; they assert against searchKnowledge's raw top similarity, not the tool-handler filter (see tests/tools.test.ts for the handler-level filter assertion). 'fallbackQueries' (issue #362) is the deliberate exception to the paraphrase-only rule above: each is an EXACT-STRING query (an identifier copied verbatim from its expectedTitle entry's content) — the input class dense sentence embeddings underweight, and exactly what searchKnowledgeLexical's word_similarity fallback exists to resolve. Asserted against searchKnowledgeLexical directly, and checked for false positives against every OTHER fixture entry (including the distractor rows above).

entries.title: Getting Anthropic API access
entries.content: To use the Anthropic API you need to sign up for a developer account at console.anthropic.com and generate an API key from the dashboard before you can make any requests.

entries.title: API rate limits
entries.content: New API accounts default to 50 requests per minute and 40000 tokens per minute; you can request a higher limit from support once you have some usage history.

entries.title: Discord server rules
entries.content: Be respectful, no spam, no self-promotion outside the showcase channel, and keep discussion on topic for each channel.

entries.title: How to link WhatsApp and Discord identities
entries.content: Message the bot with the link-whatsapp command and follow the one-time code flow to associate your WhatsApp number with your Discord account.

entries.title: Meetup schedule
entries.content: The Auckland meetup group gets together on the first Tuesday of every month at 6pm at the usual venue.

entries.title: Requesting admin role
entries.content: Admin roles are granted manually by an existing admin after you have been an active, trusted member of the community for at least a month.

entries.title: Reporting a bug in the bot
entries.content: If the bot gives a wrong or unsafe answer, post the details in the feedback channel so an admin can review and correct the underlying knowledge entry.

entries.title: Claude Agent SDK vs Anthropic API
entries.content: The Claude Agent SDK is a higher-level framework for building agents, while the raw Anthropic API is the lower-level HTTP interface that it calls under the hood.

entries.title: Exporting your conversation history
entries.content: You can ask the bot to export everything it has stored about you, and an admin will send you a copy of your interaction history within a few days.

entries.title: Deleting your data
entries.content: Members can ask the bot or an admin to permanently purge their stored messages and any knowledge entries sourced from them at any time.

entries.title: Setting up local development
entries.content: Clone the repository, copy the example environment file, install dependencies, and run the migration script against a local Postgres instance before starting the bot.

entries.title: Choosing between Discord and WhatsApp support
entries.content: Discord is best for open community discussion and searchable history, while WhatsApp suits members who prefer a phone-based, one-on-one channel.

entries.title: API key rotation policy
entries.content: Rotate your API key roughly every ninety days by generating a replacement in the console and revoking the old one once traffic has moved over.

entries.title: Community code of conduct
entries.content: All members must follow the code of conduct: be kind, no harassment of any kind, and never share someone else's private information without consent.

entries.title: Billing and usage dashboards
entries.content: Your organization's console shows current spend and token usage broken down by day, which is useful for catching unexpected cost spikes early.

entries.title: Choosing a Claude model for a new project
entries.content: Pick a smaller, faster model for high-volume simple tasks and reserve the largest model for work that genuinely needs deeper reasoning, since cost and latency scale with capability.

entries.title: Knowledge staleness window
entries.content: The bot gently flags an answer as possibly outdated once a knowledge entry has gone this many days without being edited or looked up again; admins tune that window with the KNOWLEDGE_STALE_DAYS setting so the nudge fires neither too eagerly nor too rarely.

queries.query: how do I sign up so I can start calling the API
what's the cap on how many requests I can send per minute
what are the guidelines for behaving in this server
how do I connect my phone number to my discord account
queries.expectedTitle: How to link WhatsApp and Discord identities

queries.query: when is the next in-person get-together
how does someone become a moderator here
the assistant gave me a wrong answer, who do I tell
queries.expectedTitle: Reporting a bug in the bot

queries.query: what's the practical difference between using the SDK and hitting the API myself
queries.expectedTitle: Claude Agent SDK vs Anthropic API

queries.query: can I get a copy of everything the bot has recorded about me
how do I get all my stored messages wiped for good
what are the steps to run this project on my own machine
should I reach the community through a group chat platform or a messaging app
queries.expectedTitle: Choosing between Discord and WhatsApp support

queries.query: how often should I swap out my secret key for a new one
what's expected of members in terms of treating each other well
where can I see how much I've spent so far this month
which model size makes sense for a simple high-throughput task
queries.expectedTitle: Choosing a Claude model for a new project

negativeQueries.query: what's the best coffee place near the venue
negativeQueries.label: off-topic

negativeQueries.query: is it going to rain this weekend
negativeQueries.label: off-topic

negativeQueries.query: how many weeks does it usually take to hear back after applying for a role
negativeQueries.label: near-miss: topically adjacent to 'Requesting admin role' (also about becoming an admin) but asks about timing no entry answers
